text:
Zoldyck Personal Transmitter (, Zorudikku-ke Sen'yō Musenki—lit. "Zoldyck Family/Home Private Transceiver") is a communication device used by members of the Zoldyck Family. It only rings when an assassination job is successful. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 100 

Appearance

The simple device has an oval shape and is small enough to fit in the palm of the hand. It has a side button for quick contact, a small LED status light indicator/display on the top, a small microphone and/or speaker on the bottom, and also what appears to be an earphone jack connector port for audio output underneath. Additionally, in the middle of the device, there is a hexagram (✡) stamped within a circle.

Regarding the color, in the 1999 anime adaptation, the devices are light gray/silver with the symbol area and the LED status light indicator being light blue. In the 2011 anime adaptation, it is dark blue with a golden yellow symbol area and a red LED indicator.

Plot

Yorknew City arc

After assassinating the Ten Dons, Illumi uses the transmitter to contact Silva. He asks where his client is, and Chrollo pulls himself out of the rubble from his fight against Zeno. Illumi receives confirmation that Chrollo is still alive and tells his father to inform him that they have killed the Ten Dons. As Maha and Kalluto stand nearby, Illumi asks that he wire the money to the account he mentioned earlier. 

Trivia

Hexagrams, six-pointed geometric star figures, are used in a variety of religions and cultures including: Islam* (e.g., as the Seal of Solomon); Christianity*; Occultism*; Hinduism/Buddhism/Jainism* (generally in cosmological diagrams, e.g., as the Shatkona and in the Anāhata symbol); and especially Judaism*, where the hexagram is the symbol used for "The Star of David" (✡).
Another Zoldyck-related item, Killua's yo-yos, also has the same symbol on it.
